title: Narrative Arc — Capability from Ambiguity
recognition: Transformation work routinely arrives with incomplete information, competing priorities, and unstable assumptions.
reframe: Ambiguity is not only a defect to eliminate; it can be raw material for capability construction.
framework: Name the ambiguity, separate facts from assumptions, assign decisions, define risk, create feedback loops, and preserve learning.
proof: The draft grounds the model in recurring operating conditions and distinguishes disciplined uncertainty from fabricated certainty.
application: Executives can use each ambiguous situation to improve language, decision paths, ownership, and organizational judgment.
consequence: The organization becomes better able to process the next ambiguity instead of merely surviving the current one.
invitation: Do not merely resolve ambiguity; convert it into reusable capability.
